title: System Calls
summary: Describes the system call mechanism: what it is, why it is needed, the complete 9-step sequence, and the importance of buffering for performance.
content_points: System calls let user-mode applications request privileged services from the kernel.
Implemented via software interrupts: CPU switches to kernel mode, routine executes, returns to user mode.
9 steps from parameter placement to result retrieval.
System calls are expensive (mode switch overhead); use buffers to minimise call frequency.
Example: printf buffers text locally; write() system call only when buffer is full.
learning_objectives: Explain why system calls are necessary
Describe buffering as a performance optimisation
